text:
HEMATOLOGY/ONCOLOGY SURVIVORSHIP CLINIC
FOLLOW-UP VISIT NOTE

ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: Hematology/Oncology, Bone Marrow Transplant Program

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Routine 18-month post-allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant survivorship follow-up. Patient presents for scheduled surveillance visit, ongoing late effects monitoring, and transition off immunosuppression.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Renata Kowalczyk is a 50-year-old female with a history of de novo acute myeloid leukemia (AML), FAB M4 subtype (acute myelomonocytic leukemia), diagnosed approximately 18 months prior at age 48. Her AML was molecularly characterized by a FLT3-ITD mutation carrying a high allelic ratio (VAF 0.82, >48 bp insertion), placing her in the ELN 2022 Adverse risk category. She had a co-occurring DNMT3A R882H mutation, felt to represent a pre-existing clonal hematopoiesis (CHIP) clone. NPM1, IDH1/2, and CEBPA were all wild-type. Cytogenetics showed a normal 46,XX karyotype.

She achieved a complete morphologic remission (CR) with MRD negativity (both by multiparameter flow cytometry and FLT3-ITD PCR) after a single cycle of 7+3 induction chemotherapy with cytarabine and daunorubicin, augmented by midostaurin (Rydapt) as per the RATIFY trial protocol. Given her adverse-risk disease, she was referred expeditiously for allogeneic stem cell transplantation in first CR. No fully HLA-matched sibling was available; a 10/10 allele-level matched unrelated donor (MUD) - a 38-year-old CMV-seronegative male - was identified through the NMDP. She received one cycle of bridging consolidation with intermediate-dose cytarabine (IDAC) to maintain remission during the donor workup interval.

She underwent myeloablative conditioning with fludarabine/busulfan (FluBu4) with PK-guided busulfan dosing, GVHD prophylaxis with tacrolimus/short-course MTX, and anti-thymocyte globulin (thymoglobulin) for the MUD setting. Peripheral blood stem cell infusion (CD34+ dose 6.8 x 10^6/kg) was uneventful. Early post-transplant course was notable for grade 3 mucositis, febrile neutropenia with bacteremia (E. coli during induction, negative cultures peri-transplant), mild sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (SOS) meeting Modified Seattle Criteria - resolved without defibrotide - and CMV viremia detected at day +28 (460 IU/mL) treated with valganciclovir with subsequent clearance. Neutrophil engraftment occurred at day +14, platelet engraftment at day +22.

She developed mild chronic GVHD at approximately day +100, involving skin (lichenoid plaques over forearms and shins, BSA <10%), eyes (dry eye syndrome, Schirmer test 7 mm/5 min), and oral mucosa (xerostomia). NIH global severity was rated as Mild. This was managed exclusively with topical therapies (topical tacrolimus 0.1% ointment, clobetasol 0.05% ointment for skin; cyclosporine 0.05% ophthalmic emulsion and preservative-free artificial tears for eyes; Biotene products for oral dryness), along with continuation of tacrolimus as systemic immunosuppression. She did not require systemic corticosteroids at any point.

Disease surveillance has been reassuring at every timepoint. Bone marrow biopsies at day +60 and day +365 both confirmed sustained complete morphologic and molecular remission with MRD negativity. Peripheral blood chimerism has been 100% donor since day +150. The 46,XY karyotype on marrow specimens confirms complete donor engraftment from the male donor. DNMT3A R882H ddPCR signal was undetectable on the day +365 biopsy, suggesting even the CHIP clone has been replaced by donor hematopoiesis.

At today's visit (approximately day +540, month 18 post-transplant), she presents for her scheduled milestone visit, which marks the planned endpoint of tacrolimus taper and transition off all systemic immunosuppression. She reports feeling well overall. She returned to her position as a high school biology teacher part-time at month 4 and resumed full-time work at month 5 post-transplant, which she continues. She denies fatigue beyond normal baseline, reports no new skin changes, no increased dryness in eyes or mouth, no dyspnea, no fevers, no night sweats, and no bleeding. She has had no infections requiring hospitalization or IV antibiotics since her post-transplant CMV episode. She is managing her premature ovarian insufficiency (POI) symptoms on transdermal hormone replacement therapy initiated approximately 3 months ago under reproductive endocrinology guidance, and reports meaningful improvement in vasomotor symptoms and overall quality of life.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
- Constitutional: Denies fever, chills, or night sweats. No unintentional weight loss. Reports normal energy levels for her age.
- HEENT: Mild intermittent eye dryness, well-controlled on current eye drop regimen. Denies visual changes or new photophobia. Mild residual oral dryness, improved on current regimen; no oral pain or ulceration.
- Skin: No new rashes, blistering, or skin tightening. The lichenoid plaques on forearms and shins have fully resolved per patient report. No new lesions.
- Respiratory: No dyspnea, cough, or wheezing at rest or with exertion.
- Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or lower extremity edema.
- Gastrointestinal: No nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, or diarrhea. Normal bowel habits. No rectal bleeding.
- Genitourinary: No hematuria, dysuria, or urinary frequency. No vaginal bleeding on current HRT.
- Musculoskeletal: No joint pain, swelling, or muscle weakness. No bone pain.
- Neurological: Resolving mild bilateral hand tingling (grade 1 peripheral neuropathy attributed to prior cytarabine); no progression, functionally non-limiting.
- Endocrine/Metabolic: Hot flashes and night sweats significantly improved on HRT. No polyuria or polydipsia (diabetes well-controlled per recent labs).
- Psychiatric: Mood good. No depression or anxiety reported. Patient feels "back to normal."
- Hematologic: No bleeding or bruising. No adenopathy noted by patient.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
1. Acute myeloid leukemia, FAB M4, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, DNMT3A R882H co-mutation, ELN 2022 Adverse risk - diagnosed approximately 18 months ago; in sustained complete remission post-alloHSCT (as above)
2. Type 2 diabetes mellitus - managed with metformin; tacrolimus-associated insulin resistance has been a complicating factor
3. Mild hypertension
4. Chemotherapy/conditioning-induced premature ovarian insufficiency - on HRT
5. Mild chronic GVHD (skin, eye, oral) - resolved on topical therapies
6. Post-transplant CMV viremia - treated and cleared
7. Mild SOS/VOD post-transplant - resolved spontaneously
8. Grade 1 cytarabine-associated peripheral neuropathy - mild, improving
9. Uterine fibroid (2.2 cm intramural, stable on imaging)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
High school biology teacher, currently working full-time. Lives with husband and two adult children. Non-smoker, no alcohol use reported. Good social support system. Excellent medication adherence by patient report and pill diary review.

FAMILY HISTORY:
No family history of leukemia, myelodysplastic syndrome, or other hematologic malignancies. Mother with type 2 diabetes. Father with hypertension and coronary artery disease.

ALLERGIES:
No known drug allergies.

MEDICATIONS (current at this visit):
1. Tacrolimus (Prograf) - being discontinued today per taper schedule (last dose taken this morning; patient advised not to refill)
2. Metformin 1000 mg twice daily (for type 2 diabetes)
3. Transdermal estradiol 0.05 mg/24h patch, changed twice weekly (for premature ovarian insufficiency)
4. Oral micronized progesterone 100 mg nightly (for uterine protection with HRT)
5. Cyclosporine 0.05% ophthalmic emulsion (Restasis) 1 drop each eye twice daily
6. Preservative-free artificial tears as needed (eye lubrication)
7. Calcium carbonate 600 mg twice daily with vitamin D 800 IU (for bone density)
8. Lisinopril 5 mg daily (for hypertension)
9. TMP-SMX single-strength (for PCP prophylaxis - planned for discontinuation today)
10. Acyclovir 400 mg twice daily (for HSV/VZV prophylaxis - planned for discontinuation today)
Note: Midostaurin was not restarted post-transplant per institutional protocol.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vital Signs: Blood pressure 124/78 mmHg, heart rate 72 bpm and regular, respiratory rate 16, temperature 37.0 degrees C, oxygen saturation 98% on room air, weight 65 kg (baseline pre-diagnosis 67 kg; near full weight recovery).

General: Well-appearing female, looks well and in no acute distress. Well-groomed, pleasant, engaged in discussion.

Head/Eyes/Ears/Nose/Throat: Anicteric sclerae. Conjunctivae mildly pink without injection or erythema. Mild conjunctival dryness noted on exam without active inflammation. Oropharynx: Mucosa moist (improved compared to prior visits); no ulcerations, erythema, or candidal plaques. No trismus. Dentition intact, no obvious caries at today's visual inspection. No cervical lymphadenopathy.

Skin: No rash. Prior forearm and shin lichenoid involvement fully resolved; faint post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation remains on bilateral forearms (expected residual finding, no active disease). No new plaques, papules, or bullae. No sclerodermatous changes. No jaundice.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No lower extremity edema.

Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes, crackles, or rhonchi.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly. No splenomegaly. Normal bowel sounds. No ascites.

Neurological: Alert and fully oriented. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Motor strength 5/5 throughout. Deep tendon reflexes 2+ and symmetric. Mild bilateral decreased light touch sensation in fingertips (consistent with residual grade 1 peripheral neuropathy, stable and non-progressive).

Musculoskeletal: No synovitis, joint swelling, or restricted range of motion. Gait normal.

Lymph Nodes: No palpable lymphadenopathy in cervical, axillary, or inguinal regions.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (obtained today):
Complete Blood Count:
  - WBC: 7,100/microliter (normal)
  - ANC: 4,800/microliter (normal)
  - Hemoglobin: 13.1 g/dL (normal for donor male karyotype; excellent recovery)
  - Platelets: 204,000/microliter (normal)
  - MCV: 89 fL (normal); no cytopenias.

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
  - Sodium 139 mEq/L, Potassium 4.0 mEq/L, Chloride 104 mEq/L, Bicarbonate 24 mEq/L
  - BUN 16 mg/dL, Creatinine 0.92 mg/dL
  - eGFR: 70 mL/min/1.73m2 (improved from nadir of 64 mL/min at month 12, consistent with tacrolimus dose reduction; now approaching normal range)
  - Glucose 108 mg/dL (fasting)
  - ALT 22 U/L, AST 19 U/L, alkaline phosphatase 68 U/L (all normal)
  - Total bilirubin 0.6 mg/dL (normal)
  - Albumin 4.2 g/dL (normal)
  - Total protein 7.1 g/dL (normal)

Tacrolimus trough (morning, prior to today's final dose): 3.6 ng/mL (appropriately low, per tapering schedule; planned for discontinuation today).

HbA1c: 7.0% (good glycemic control; stable or slightly improved from 7.2% at month 12, despite tacrolimus effects which will now lessen).

TSH: 1.8 mIU/L (normal thyroid function).

Peripheral blood chimerism (STR analysis, sent this visit, results pending - prior value at day +150 was 100% donor): Pending; expected to confirm 100% donor engraftment.

CMV PCR (plasma): Undetectable (most recent of multiple undetectable results).

EBV PCR: Undetectable.

---

IMAGING RESULTS:
Most recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (day +270): No evidence of extramedullary AML, no pathologic lymphadenopathy, normal spleen size (9.8 cm), stable uterine fibroid (2.2 cm). No new findings of concern. No surveillance imaging is scheduled today; next CT will be obtained at the 2-year mark per protocol.

DXA bone density scan (performed at 18 months per survivorship protocol, result reviewed today): Lumbar spine Z-score -0.8, femoral neck Z-score -0.9. Compared to prior DXA at day +365 (Z-score -1.1 spine), there is a meaningful improvement in bone mineral density on calcium/vitamin D supplementation. No bisphosphonate therapy indicated at this time; continue supplementation and monitoring.

Ophthalmology note (recent visit, reviewed today): Mild posterior subcapsular cataract left eye, attributed to prior steroid exposure (induction/consolidation glucocorticoids and tacrolimus). Stable, visually insignificant at this time. Annual monitoring recommended. Dry eye disease well-controlled on Restasis and lubricating drops; no corneal staining.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

Ms. Kowalczyk is a 50-year-old woman doing exceptionally well at 18 months following myeloablative matched unrelated donor allogeneic stem cell transplant for high-risk AML (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN Adverse). She is in sustained complete remission with MRD negativity and 100% donor chimerism at all assessments. Her chronic GVHD (mild, multi-organ) has resolved fully on topical therapies alone, without the need for systemic corticosteroids. Her blood counts are entirely normal and her organ function has improved with tacrolimus reduction. Today marks a significant milestone: discontinuation of all systemic immunosuppression.

1. AML - Sustained complete remission, MRD negative, 100% donor chimerism:
   - No clinical, laboratory, or imaging evidence of relapse at this visit.
   - Continue surveillance per transplant protocol: Next bone marrow biopsy planned at day +730 (2 years post-transplant); peripheral blood chimerism check every 3 months.
   - Patient counseled on signs and symptoms of relapse (new cytopenias, fatigue, fevers, bleeding, lymphadenopathy) and instructed to contact the clinic immediately if these develop.
   - Late relapse risk remains low but not zero, and surveillance is maintained accordingly.
   - No FLT3 inhibitor maintenance indicated post-transplant at this center at this time.

2. Tacrolimus discontinuation - Milestone:
   - Patient has completed the planned 18-month tacrolimus taper. Tacrolimus is being discontinued as of today.
   - Excellent tolerance throughout; no clinically significant tacrolimus toxicity beyond mild nephrotoxicity (now improving).
   - Patient counseled on symptoms of GVHD flare following immunosuppression withdrawal (skin rash, diarrhea, elevated LFTs, worsening dry eyes/mouth), and instructed to call with any concerns. She is being given a clear taper card and emergency contact information.

3. Infectious prophylaxis discontinuation:
   - TMP-SMX (PCP prophylaxis) discontinued today, consistent with standard practice of discontinuing prophylaxis following immunosuppression withdrawal in the absence of active GVHD.
   - Acyclovir (HSV/VZV prophylaxis) discontinued today for the same rationale.
   - CMV monitoring: Will obtain CMV PCR at next visit in 3 months given CMV+/- donor-recipient pairing; risk of late reactivation is low but present. No pre-emptive treatment currently warranted.
   - Patient counseled on varicella-zoster reactivation (shingles) risk and instructed to seek prompt evaluation for any dermatomal pain/rash. Recombinant zoster vaccine (Shingrix) planned to be administered once immune reconstitution is confirmed (typically at 24 months post-transplant in this protocol).

4. Chronic GVHD - Resolved:
   - Skin: Lichenoid plaques fully resolved; only post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation remains. Topical tacrolimus and clobetasol ointment discontinued as of last visit.
   - Eyes: Well-controlled on Restasis and lubricating drops. Continue cyclosporine ophthalmic emulsion and preservative-free artificial tears as needed for comfort. Annual ophthalmology follow-up.
   - Oral: Xerostomia resolved; Biotene use optional for comfort. Reminder for dental evaluation every 6 months given prior cGVHD oral involvement and ongoing caries risk.
   - NIH global cGVHD severity: Resolved/remission.

5. Premature Ovarian Insufficiency:
   - Patient doing well on transdermal estradiol and oral micronized progesterone. Significant symptomatic improvement with HRT. Continue current regimen under ongoing reproductive endocrinology co-management.
   - Annual gynecologic follow-up maintained.

6. Type 2 Diabetes:
   - HbA1c 7.0%, well-controlled. Tacrolimus discontinuation may further improve insulin sensitivity. Metformin continued at current dose.
   - Will recheck HbA1c at 3-month follow-up; if improved, discuss with primary care whether dose optimization is warranted.
   - eGFR now 70 mL/min/1.73m2 - metformin is safe to continue at this level.

7. Bone Health:
   - Improving BMD on calcium/vitamin D supplementation. Z-scores borderline low but trending toward normal. No bisphosphonate required at this time.
   - Repeat DXA in 12 months.
   - Advised on weight-bearing exercise, dietary calcium, and sun exposure (with appropriate photoprotection given prior cGVHD skin history).

8. Cataract Monitoring:
   - Mild posterior subcapsular cataract left eye - stable and visually insignificant. Annual ophthalmology follow-up in place.

9. Hypertension:
   - Well-controlled on lisinopril 5 mg daily. Blood pressure at goal today. Continue current management; lisinopril also provides renal protection. Renal function improving.

10. Peripheral Neuropathy:
    - Grade 1 bilateral hand tingling from prior cytarabine, stable and non-progressive. No further workup needed at this time. If worsening, consider neurology referral and EMG/NCS.

11. Surveillance Imaging:
    - CT CAP at 2-year post-transplant mark (approximately 6 months from now).
    - No urgent imaging indicated today.

12. Vaccinations:
    - Immunization program ongoing per 18-month post-transplant schedule reviewed today. Today: MMR vaccine administered (appropriate timing given immunosuppression discontinuation; confirmed seroconversion schedule to be checked at 3 months). Varicella vaccine and live vaccines held per protocol until 24 months and immunosuppression-free status confirmed.
    - Recombinant zoster vaccine (Shingrix) planned at 24-month visit.
    - Ensure influenza vaccination annually; COVID-19 booster to be addressed per current guidelines.

13. Secondary Malignancy Surveillance:
    - Counseled on elevated long-term risk of secondary malignancies after alloHSCT and immunosuppression (skin cancers, EBV-related lymphoproliferative disorders, others). Advised strict sun protection. Annual skin exam by dermatology initiated. EBV PCR currently undetectable.
    - Age-appropriate cancer screening: Cervical (Pap/HPV co-test per interval), breast (annual mammography - scheduled), colorectal (colonoscopy at standard screening interval; none yet required at age 50 given no family history of CRC).

14. Quality of Life and Psychosocial:
    - Patient is fully reintegrated into professional and personal life. Excellent functional status (ECOG PS 0). Expressed gratitude and optimism. Brief PHQ-2 administered - score 0 (no depression screen positive).
    - Survivorship care plan updated and provided to patient. Copy sent to primary care physician.
    - Next appointment: 3 months (routine bloodwork, chimerism result review, GVHD surveillance post-immunosuppression withdrawal). Long-term plan: Annual visits at 2-year, 3-year, 5-year marks; primary care co-management of late effects.

Overall, this is an outstanding outcome for a patient who presented with high-risk FLT3-ITD AML 18 months ago and has navigated induction therapy, allogeneic transplant, CMV viremia, and mild cGVHD successfully. She is off immunosuppression, in confirmed molecular remission, and living an active, full life. Her prognosis is now genuinely favorable.

---

Time spent with patient: 55 minutes (>50% of time spent in counseling and coordination).

Electronically signed: Attending Physician, Bone Marrow Transplant/Hematologic Oncology
